NKJV.1: I love the LORD, because He has heard My voice and my supplications.
NKJV.2: Because He has inclined His ear to me, Therefore I will call upon Him as long as I live.
NKJV.3: The pains of death surrounded me, And the pangs of Sheol laid hold of me; I found trouble and sorrow.
NKJV.4: Then I called upon the name of the LORD: “O LORD, I implore You, deliver my soul!”
NKJV.5: Gracious is the LORD, and righteous; Yes, our God is merciful.
NKJV.6: The LORD preserves the simple; I was brought low, and He saved me.
NKJV.7: Return to your rest, O my soul, For the LORD has dealt bountifully with you.
NKJV.8: For You have delivered my soul from death, My eyes from tears, And my feet from falling.
NKJV.9: I will walk before the LORD  In the land of the living.
NKJV.10: I believed, therefore I spoke, “I am greatly afflicted.”
NKJV.11: I said in my haste, “All men are liars.”
NKJV.12: What shall I render to the LORD For all His benefits toward me?
NKJV.13: I will take up the cup of salvation, And call upon the name of the LORD.
NKJV.14: I will pay my vows to the LORD Now in the presence of all His people.
NKJV.15: Precious in the sight of the LORD Is the death of His saints.
NKJV.16: O LORD, truly I am Your servant; I am Your servant, the son of Your maidservant; You have loosed my bonds.
NKJV.17: I will offer to You the sacrifice of thanksgiving, And will call upon the name of the LORD.
NKJV.18: I will pay my vows to the LORD Now in the presence of all His people,
NKJV.19: In the courts of the LORD’s house, In the midst of you, O Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!

ESV.1: I love the LORD, because he has heard my voice and my pleas for mercy.
ESV.2: Because he inclined his ear to me, therefore I will call on him as long as I live.
ESV.3: The snares of death encompassed me; the pangs of Sheol laid hold on me; I suffered distress and anguish.
ESV.4: Then I called on the name of the LORD: “O LORD, I pray, deliver my soul!”
ESV.5: Gracious is the LORD, and righteous; our God is merciful.
ESV.6: The LORD preserves the simple; when I was brought low, he saved me.
ESV.7: Return, O my soul, to your rest; for the LORD has dealt bountifully with you.
ESV.8: For you have delivered my soul from death, my eyes from tears, my feet from stumbling;
ESV.9: I will walk before the LORD in the land of the living.
ESV.10: I believed, even when  I spoke: “I am greatly afflicted”;
ESV.11: I said in my alarm, “All mankind are liars.”
ESV.12: What shall I render to the LORD for all his benefits to me?
ESV.13: I will lift up the cup of salvation and call on the name of the LORD,
ESV.14: I will pay my vows to the LORD in the presence of all his people.
ESV.15: Precious in the sight of the LORD is the death of his saints.
ESV.16: O LORD, I am your servant; I am your servant, the son of your maidservant. You have loosed my bonds.
ESV.17: I will offer to you the sacrifice of thanksgiving and call on the name of the LORD.
ESV.18: I will pay my vows to the LORD in the presence of all his people,
ESV.19: in the courts of the house of the LORD, in your midst, O Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!

NASB.1: I love the LORD, because He hears My voice [and] my supplications.
NASB.2: Because He has inclined His ear to me, Therefore I shall call [upon Him] as long as I live.
NASB.3: The cords of death encompassed me And the terrors of Sheol came upon me; I found distress and sorrow.
NASB.4: Then I called upon the name of the LORD: 'O LORD, I beseech You, save my life!'
NASB.5: Gracious is the LORD, and righteous; Yes, our God is compassionate.
NASB.6: The LORD preserves the simple; I was brought low, and He saved me.
NASB.7: Return to your rest, O my soul, For the LORD has dealt bountifully with you.
NASB.8: For You have rescued my soul from death, My eyes from tears, My feet from stumbling.
NASB.9: I shall walk before the LORD In the land of the living.
NASB.10: I believed when I said, 'I am greatly afflicted.'
NASB.11: I said in my alarm, 'All men are liars.'
NASB.12: What shall I render to the LORD For all His benefits toward me?
NASB.13: I shall lift up the cup of salvation And call upon the name of the LORD.
NASB.14: I shall pay my vows to the LORD, Oh [may it be] in the presence of all His people.
NASB.15: Precious in the sight of the LORD Is the death of His godly ones.
NASB.16: O LORD, surely I am Your servant, I am Your servant, the son of Your handmaid, You have loosed my bonds.
NASB.17: To You I shall offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving, And call upon the name of the LORD.
NASB.18: I shall pay my vows to the LORD, Oh [may it be] in the presence of all His people,
NASB.19: In the courts of the LORD'S house, In the midst of you, O Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!

NLT.1:
I love the LORD because he hears my voice
and my prayer for mercy.
NLT.2:
Because he bends down to listen,
I will pray as long as I have breath!
NLT.3:
Death wrapped its ropes around me;
the terrors of the grave overtook me.
I saw only trouble and sorrow.
NLT.4:
Then I called on the name of the LORD:
“Please, LORD, save me!”
NLT.5:
How kind the LORD is! How good he is!
So merciful, this God of ours!
NLT.6:
The LORD protects those of childlike faith;
I was facing death, and he saved me.
NLT.7:
Let my soul be at rest again,
for the LORD has been good to me.
NLT.8:
He has saved me from death,
my eyes from tears,
my feet from stumbling.
NLT.9:
And so I walk in the LORD’s presence
as I live here on earth!
NLT.10:
I believed in you, so I said,
“I am deeply troubled, LORD.”
NLT.11:
In my anxiety I cried out to you,
“These people are all liars!”
NLT.12:
What can I offer the LORD
for all he has done for me?
NLT.13:
I will lift up the cup of salvation
and praise the LORD’s name for saving me.
NLT.14:
I will keep my promises to the LORD
in the presence of all his people.
NLT.15:
The LORD cares deeply
when his loved ones die.
NLT.16:
O LORD, I am your servant;
yes, I am your servant, born into your household;
you have freed me from my chains.
NLT.17:
I will offer you a sacrifice of thanksgiving
and call on the name of the LORD.
NLT.18:
I will fulfill my vows to the LORD
in the presence of all his people —
NLT.19:
in the house of the LORD
in the heart of Jerusalem.
Praise the LORD!

WEB.1: I love Yahweh, because he listens to my voice, and my cries for mercy.
WEB.2: Because he has turned his ear to me, therefore I will call on him as long as I live.
WEB.3: The cords of death surrounded me, the pains of Sheol  got a hold of me. I found trouble and sorrow.
WEB.4: Then I called on Yahweh’s name: “Yahweh, I beg you, deliver my soul.”
WEB.5: Yahweh is Gracious and righteous. Yes, our God is merciful.
WEB.6: Yahweh preserves the simple. I was brought low, and he saved me.
WEB.7: Return to your rest, my soul, for Yahweh has dealt bountifully with you.
WEB.8: For you have delivered my soul from death, my eyes from tears, and my feet from falling.
WEB.9: I will walk before Yahweh in the land of the living.
WEB.10: I believed, therefore I said, “I was greatly afflicted.”
WEB.11: I said in my haste, “All men are liars.”
WEB.12: What will I give to Yahweh for all his benefits toward me?
WEB.13: I will take the cup of salvation, and call on Yahweh’s name.
WEB.14: I will pay my vows to Yahweh, yes, in the presence of all his people.
WEB.15: Precious in Yahweh’s sight is the death of his saints.
WEB.16: Yahweh, truly I am your servant. I am your servant, the son of your servant. You have freed me from my chains.
WEB.17: I will offer to you the sacrifice of thanksgiving, and will call on Yahweh’s name.
WEB.18: I will pay my vows to Yahweh, yes, in the presence of all his people,
WEB.19: in the courts of Yahweh’s house, in the middle of you, Jerusalem. Praise Yah!

CSB17.1:
I love the LORD because he has heard
my appeal for mercy. ⓛ
CSB17.2:
Because he has turned his ear to me,
I will call out to him as long as I live. ⓜ
CSB17.3:
The ropes of death were wrapped around me,
and the torments of Sheol overcame me;
I encountered trouble and sorrow. ⓝ
CSB17.4:
Then I called on the name of the LORD:
“LORD, save me!” ⓐ
CSB17.5:
The LORD is gracious and righteous;
our God is compassionate. ⓑ
CSB17.6:
The LORD guards the inexperienced;
I was helpless, and he saved me. ⓒ
CSB17.7:
Return to your rest, my soul,
for the LORD has been good to you. ⓓ
CSB17.8:
For you, LORD, rescued me from death,
my eyes from tears,
my feet from stumbling. ⓔ
CSB17.9:
I will walk before the LORD
in the land of the living. ⓕ
CSB17.10:
I believed, even when I said,
“I am severely oppressed.” ⓖ
CSB17.11:
In my alarm I said,
“Everyone is a liar.” ⓗ
CSB17.12:
How can I repay the LORD
for all the good he has done for me? ⓘ
CSB17.13:
I will take the cup of salvation
and call on the name of the LORD. ⓙ
CSB17.14:
I will fulfill my vows to the LORD
in the presence of all his people. ⓚ
CSB17.15:
The death of his faithful ones
is valuable in the LORD’s sight. ⓛ
CSB17.16:
LORD, I am indeed your servant;
I am your servant, the son of your female servant.
You have loosened my bonds. ⓜ
CSB17.17:
I will offer you a sacrifice of thanksgiving
and call on the name of the LORD. ⓝ
CSB17.18:
I will fulfill my vows to the LORD
in the presence of all his people, ⓐ
CSB17.19:
in the courts of the LORD’s house —
within you, Jerusalem. ⓑ
Hallelujah!

AMP.1:
I love the LORD, because He hears [and continues to hear]
My voice and my supplications (my pleas, my cries, my specific needs).
AMP.2:
Because He has inclined His ear to me,
Therefore I will call on Him as long as I live.
AMP.3:
The cords and sorrows of death encompassed me,
And the terrors of Sheol came upon me;
I found distress and sorrow.
AMP.4:
Then I called on the name of the LORD:
"O LORD, please save my life!"
AMP.5:
Gracious is the LORD, and [consistently] righteous;
Yes, our God is compassionate.
AMP.6:
The LORD protects the simple (childlike);
I was brought low [humbled and discouraged], and He saved me.
AMP.7:
Return to your rest, O my soul,
For the LORD has dealt bountifully with you.
AMP.8:
For You have rescued my life from death,
My eyes from tears,
And my feet from stumbling and falling.
AMP.9:
I will walk [in submissive wonder] before the LORD
In the land of the living.
AMP.10:
I believed [and clung to my God] when I said,
"I am greatly afflicted."
AMP.11:
I said in my alarm,
"All men are liars."
AMP.12:
What will I give to the LORD [in return]
For all His benefits toward me?
[How can I repay Him for His precious blessings?]
AMP.13:
I will lift up the cup of salvation
And call on the name of the LORD.
AMP.14:
I will pay my vows to the LORD,
Yes, in the presence of all His people.
AMP.15:
Precious [and of great consequence] in the sight of the LORD
Is the death of His godly ones [so He watches over them].
AMP.16:
O LORD, truly I am Your servant;
I am Your servant, the son of Your handmaid;
You have unfastened my chains.
AMP.17:
I will offer to You the sacrifice of thanksgiving,
And will call on the name of the LORD.
AMP.18:
I will pay my vows to the LORD,
Yes, in the presence of all His people,
AMP.19:
In the courts of the Lord's house (temple)—
In the midst of you, O Jerusalem.
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)

MSG.1:
I love God because he listened to me,
listened as I begged for mercy.
MSG.2:
He listened so intently
as I laid out my case before him.
MSG.3:
Death stared me in the face,
hell was hard on my heels.
Up against it, I didn't know which way to turn;
MSG.4:
then I called out to God for help:
"Please, God!" I cried out.
"Save my life!"
MSG.5:
God is gracious—it is he who makes things right,
our most compassionate God.
MSG.6:
God takes the side of the helpless;
when I was at the end of my rope, he saved me.
MSG.7:
I said to myself, "Relax and rest.
God has showered you with blessings.
MSG.8:
Soul, you've been rescued from death;
Eye, you've been rescued from tears;
And you, Foot, were kept from stumbling."
MSG.9:
I'm striding in the presence of God,
alive in the land of the living!
MSG.10:
I stayed faithful, though bedeviled,
and despite a ton of bad luck,
MSG.11:
Despite giving up on the human race,
saying, "They're all liars and cheats."
MSG.12:
What can I give back to God
for the blessings he's poured out on me?
MSG.13:
I'll lift high the cup of salvation—a toast to God!
I'll pray in the name of God;
MSG.14:
I'll complete what I promised God I'd do,
and I'll do it together with his people.
MSG.15:
When they arrive at the gates of death,
God welcomes those who love him.
MSG.16:
Oh, God, here I am, your servant,
your faithful servant: set me free for your service!
MSG.17:
I'm ready to offer the thanksgiving sacrifice
and pray in the name of God.
MSG.18:
I'll complete what I promised God I'd do,
and I'll do it in company with his people,
MSG.19:
In the place of worship, in God's house,
in Jerusalem, God's city.
Hallelujah!

NIV.1:
Psalm 116
I love the Lord, for he heard my voice;
he heard my cry for mercy.
NIV.2:
Because he turned his ear to me,
I will call on him as long as I live.
NIV.3:
The cords of death entangled me,
the anguish of the grave came upon me;
I was overcome by trouble and sorrow.
NIV.4:
Then I called on the name of the Lord:
“O Lord, save me!”
NIV.5:
The Lord is gracious and righteous;
our God is full of compassion.
NIV.6:
The Lord protects the simplehearted;
when I was in great need, he saved me.
NIV.7:
Be at rest once more, O my soul,
for the Lord has been good to you.
NIV.8:
For you, O Lord, have delivered my soul from death,
my eyes from tears,
my feet from stumbling,
NIV.9:
that I may walk before the Lord
in the land of the living.
NIV.10:
I believed; therefore I said,
“I am greatly afflicted.”
NIV.11:
And in my dismay I said,
“All men are liars.”
NIV.12:
How can I repay the Lord
for all his goodness to me?
NIV.13:
I will lift up the cup of salvation
and call on the name of the Lord.
NIV.14:
I will fulfill my vows to the Lord
in the presence of all his people.
NIV.15:
Precious in the sight of the Lord
is the death of his saints.
NIV.16:
O Lord, truly I am your servant;
I am your servant, the son of your maidservant;
you have freed me from my chains.
NIV.17:
I will sacrifice a thank offering to you
and call on the name of the Lord.
NIV.18:
I will fulfill my vows to the Lord
in the presence of all his people,
NIV.19:
in the courts of the house of the Lord—
in your midst, O Jerusalem.
Praise the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
I love the LORD, because he has heard
my voice and my supplications.
NRSVCE.2:
Because he inclined his ear to me,
therefore I will call on him as long as I live.
NRSVCE.3:
The snares of death encompassed me;
the pangs of Sheol laid hold on me;
I suffered distress and anguish.
NRSVCE.4:
Then I called on the name of the LORD:
“O LORD, I pray, save my life!”
NRSVCE.5:
Gracious is the LORD, and righteous;
our God is merciful.
NRSVCE.6:
The LORD protects the simple;
when I was brought low, he saved me.
NRSVCE.7:
Return, O my soul, to your rest,
for the LORD has dealt bountifully with you.
NRSVCE.8:
For you have delivered my soul from death,
my eyes from tears,
my feet from stumbling.
NRSVCE.9:
I walk before the LORD
in the land of the living.
NRSVCE.10:
I kept my faith, even when I said,
“I am greatly afflicted”;
NRSVCE.11:
I said in my consternation,
“Everyone is a liar.”
NRSVCE.12:
What shall I return to the LORD
for all his bounty to me?
NRSVCE.13:
I will lift up the cup of salvation
and call on the name of the LORD,
NRSVCE.14:
I will pay my vows to the LORD
in the presence of all his people.
NRSVCE.15:
Precious in the sight of the LORD
is the death of his faithful ones.
NRSVCE.16:
O LORD, I am your servant;
I am your servant, the child of your serving girl.
You have loosed my bonds.
NRSVCE.17:
I will offer to you a thanksgiving sacrifice
and call on the name of the LORD.
NRSVCE.18:
I will pay my vows to the LORD
in the presence of all his people,
NRSVCE.19:
in the courts of the house of the LORD,
in your midst, O Jerusalem.
Praise the LORD!

NET.1: I love the LORD because he heard my plea for mercy,
NET.2: and listened to me. As long as I live, I will call to him when I need help.
NET.3: The ropes of death tightened around me, the snares of Sheol confronted me. I was confronted with trouble and sorrow.
NET.4: I called on the name of the LORD, “Please LORD, rescue my life!”
NET.5: The LORD is merciful and fair; our God is compassionate.
NET.6: The LORD protects the untrained; I was in serious trouble and he delivered me.
NET.7: Rest once more, my soul, for the LORD has vindicated you.
NET.8: Yes, LORD, you rescued my life from death, and kept my feet from stumbling.
NET.9: I will serve the LORD in the land of the living.
NET.10: I had faith when I said, “I am severely oppressed.”
NET.11: I rashly declared, “All men are liars.”
NET.12: How can I repay the LORD for all his acts of kindness to me?
NET.13: I will celebrate my deliverance, and call on the name of the LORD.
NET.14: I will fulfill my vows to the LORD before all his people.
NET.15: The LORD values the lives of his faithful followers.
NET.16: Yes, LORD! I am indeed your servant; I am your lowest slave. You saved me from death.
NET.17: I will present a thank offering to you, and call on the name of the LORD.
NET.18: I will fulfill my vows to the LORD before all his people,
NET.19: in the courts of the LORD’s temple, in your midst, O Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!
